title: slash pages
excerpt: A guide to common pages you can add to your website
content_html:
A guide to common pages you can add to your website

Slash pages are common pages you can add to your website, usually with a standard, root-level slug like /now, /about, or /uses. They tend to describe the individual behind the site and are distinguishing characteristics of the IndieWeb.

The name was coined independently by both Caleb Hearth and Shellsharks. This page was made by Robb Knight.

# /about

a page all about you

  • /about on IndieWeb
  • Directory: aboutideasnow.com

# /ai

a page on your website that describes how you use, or don't use, AI

  • Creator: Damola Morenikeji
  • Directory: docs.google.com

# /blank

a page left intentionally blank

  • /blank on IndieWeb

# /blogroll

a list of other sites that you read, are a follower of, or recommend

  • /blogroll on IndieWeb
  • Directory: blogroll.org

Similar to /podroll, but for blogs, and /postroll

# /calendar

a list of dates from movies, tv shows, culture, that you observe

  • Creator: Robb Knight

# /canon

an encapsulation, in list form, of those things that have most shaped you. A sort of annotated bibliography of influences.

  • /canon on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Brendan Schlagel

# /carry

a list of your every day carry items

Everyday Carry on Wikipedia

Similar to /uses

# /changelog or /log

a summary of changes to your website

  • /changelog on IndieWeb

# /chipotle

You should make a /chipotle page so when a friend is picking up Chipotle for you, you can send them a link instead of having to redescribe/retype your Chipotle order

  • /chipotle on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Shellsharks

# /colophon

a page that describes how the site is made, with what tools, supporting what technologies

  • /colophon on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Samuel Palmer

a page outlining what a community is about and its values

  • Creator: Adam Newbold

# /concerts

a list of the concerts you've been to.

  • /concerts on IndieWeb
  • @lachlanjc/concerts
  • @jaspermayone/concerts

a way for people to contact you

  • /contact on IndieWeb

Similar to /hello

# /daylists

A list of Spotify Daylists that have been created for you in the past

  • /daylists on IndieWeb
  • Creator: James

# /death

a page with instructions for family and friends in case of your demise

  • Creator: toby3d

# /defaults

a list of the apps you uses for various tasks, usually based on the list from Hemispheric Views 097

  • Creator: Hemispheric Views
  • Directory: defaults.rknight.me

May also be covered with /uses

# /disability

a page describing your disabilities and how it affects you

  • Creator: Vincent Lammens

# /elsewhere or /appearances

a list of output or writings on sites other than one's own

  • /elsewhere on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Declan Chidlow

# /follow or /subscribe or /feeds

a page listing your RSS/Atom/JSON feeds and other way to subscribe to your posts

  • /follow on IndieWeb

# /for or /dedication

a page where the author honors their loved ones, or thanks someone who influenced or supported the work

  • Creator: Bino

# /glossary or /lexicon or /phrased

a list of words you often use and their meanings, so your readers can understand them the same way you do. (or simply because you want to list your favorite words!)

  • Creator: Eltrac

# /green

a page that describes how author/creator contributes positively to environment

  • Creator: Mat B

# /guy or /girl or /bloke or /gal or /your-pronoun-here

“I’m an insert interest here guy!”

  • Creator: Andrew Canion

# /hats

your hats

  • /hats on IndieWeb
  • Creator: James

# /hello

a single page listing the ways that you prefer to keep in touch

  • Creator: Alastair Johnston
  • Directory: alastairjohnston.com

Similar to /contact

# /hills or /hill

a list of stupid hills you're willing to die on

  • Creator: nemecle

# /ideas

things people want to make, or concepts they are considering

  • /ideas on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Artem Tyurin
  • Directory: aboutideasnow.com

# /interests

a place for website authors to list out things they’re passionate about and expound on what it is about those things that excites them

  • /interests on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Chris Burnell
  • Directory: chrisburnell.github.io

# /junk or /sale or /buy

host an indie-web yardsale

  • Creator: Taylor

Related to /wish

# /languages

A list of languages you speak, understand, write, or are learning

  • Creator: Christopher Kirk-Nielsen

# /links or /bookmarks

a list of links to sites you want link to, share, or bookmark for future reference

  • Directory: bukmark.club

Should be marked up with the u-bookmark-of property if you're implementing microformats.

Note: /links is also commonly used for a list of links to the website owners other sites and online accounts

# /next

What are you going to do next

  • Creator: Robb Knight

Similar to /someday

# /nope

a list of things you don't like or don't want to do

  • Creator: Jack Baty

# /now

a page that tells you what this person is focused on at this point in their life

  • /now on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Derek Sivers
  • Directories:
    • nownownow.com
    • now.garden
    • aboutideasnow.com

# /pfp

a timeline of all your profile pictures

  • Creator: Kieran Klukas

# /podroll

a list of podcasts that you recommend

  • Creator: Robb Knight

Similar to /blogroll, but for podcasts

# /posse

a page outlining your strategy for POSSE/cross-posting

  • /posse on IndieWeb

# /postroll

a list of interesting or impactful blog posts you've read

  • Creator: Brandon

Similar to /blogroll and /links

# /pricing

a page for those that want to buy your products so they can know the cost

  • Creator: Paul Esch-Laurent

Similar to /tip, but for products/services.

# /questions

a page of questions you're asking or thinking about

  • Creator: Joshua P. Steele

# /save or /Referrals

a list of referral links and discount codes for things you recommend

  • /save on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Andrew Canion

# /self-hosting or /self-hosted or /hosted

a page listing the services your hosting or managing yourself

  • Creator: Matze

# /shoebox

A bit of website where for the odds-and-ends. Put things here to delete them from other places.

  • Creator: Giles Turnbull

# /since

a habit tracker showing the time since you started doing something, or since you stopped

  • Creator: Ed Leeman

# /sitemap

list of pages on a website

  • /sitemap on IndieWeb

See also: XML sitemaps

# /slashes

meta - a list of all your slash pages

  • Creator: fLaMEd

# /someday

things you someday would like to achieve, accomplish, explore, experience, create, learn, understand

  • Creator: Alexander Sandberg

Example /someday page

Similar to /next

# /style

a personal style guide - editorial standards, rules for titles, and so forth. Could also be for a design style guide

  • /style on IndieWeb
  • Ruben Schade's style guide
  • Micah R Ledbetter's style guide
  • Robb's design style guide

# /til or /ways

A collection of concise write-ups on small things you've learnt day to day

  • /til on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Josh Branchaud

These are typically developer-focused but they don't have to be. /ways is a similar concept

# /tip or /pay or /coffee

a page so those that enjoy your work can tip you or buy you a coffee

  • /tip on IndieWeb

# /top4 or /topfour

a /top4 page is a personal webpage where you can share your definitive ranked list of your top 3 favorites and an honorable mention. In a specific topic, such as movies, albums, snacks, games, or anything else you feel strongly about.

  • /top4 on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Peter's Path
  • Directory: topfour.net

# /uses

details of the things you use on a daily basis

  • /uses on IndieWeb
  • Creator: Wes Bos
  • Directory: uses.tech

# /verify

a page on your website to list and prove your online identities

  • Creator: Molly White

# /where

a place to let others know about your current location

  • Creator: Niq

Similar to /now

# /why

an opportunity to say what you care about, what direction you’re heading, and how you hope to exist in the world. A minifesto on the purpose of your site, or your work

  • Creator: Mia

# /wish

Sharing your wishlist gives people opportunities to connect with you. From your list, they can infer passions and goals and sometimes even insecurities. They can quickly find common ground with you. They can offer suggestions and support.

  • Creator: Taylor

# /yep

a list of things you agree with or will do

  • Creator: fyr

sentences.zh: 可添加到网站的常用页面指南

blocks.html_en: Slash pages are common pages you can add to your website, usually with a standard, root-level slug like /now, /about, or /uses. They tend to describe the individual behind the site and are distinguishing characteristics of the IndieWeb.

sentences.en: Slash pages are common pages you can add to your website, usually with a standard, root-level slug like /now, /about, or /uses.
sentences.zh: 斜杠页面是您可以添加到网站的常见页面，通常使用标准的根级标签，例如 /now、/about 或 /uses。

sentences.en: They tend to describe the individual behind the site and are distinguishing characteristics of the IndieWeb.
sentences.zh: 它们倾向于描述网站背后的个人，并且是 IndieWeb 的显着特征。

blocks.html_en: The name was coined independently by both Caleb Hearth and Shellsharks. This page was made by Robb Knight.

sentences.en: The name was coined independently by both Caleb Hearth and Shellsharks.

sentences.en: This page was made by Robb Knight.
sentences.zh: 此页面由 Robb Knight 制作。

content_html_zh:
可添加到网站的常用页面指南

斜杠页面是您可以添加到网站的常见页面，通常使用标准的根级标签，例如 /now、/about 或 /uses。 它们倾向于描述网站背后的个人，并且是 IndieWeb 的显着特征。

这个名字是由 Caleb Hearth 和 Shellsharks 独立创造的。 此页面由 Robb Knight 制作。

# /about

一个关于你的页面
  • /关于 IndieWeb
  • 目录：aboutideasnow.com

# /ai

您网站上的一个页面，描述您如何使用或不使用人工智能
  • 创作者：达莫拉·莫尼克吉
  • 目录：docs.google.com

# /blank

故意留白的页面
  • IndieWeb 上的 /blank

# /blogroll

您读过、关注或推荐的其他网站的列表
  • IndieWeb 上的 /blogroll
  • 目录：blogroll.org

与 /podroll 类似，但用于博客，以及 /postroll

＃ /日历

您观察到的电影、电视节目、文化的日期列表
  • 创作者：罗伯·奈特

# /canon

以列表形式概括那些对你影响最大的事情。 一种带注释的影响参考书目。
  • IndieWeb 上的 /canon
  • 创作者：布伦丹·施拉格尔

# /carry

您每天携带的物品清单

维基百科上的日常携带

类似于 /uses

# /changelog 或 /log

您网站的更改摘要
  • IndieWeb 上的 /changelog

#/墨西哥辣椒

您应该创建一个 /chipotle 页面，这样当朋友为您挑选 Chipotle 时，您可以向他们发送链接，而不必重新描述/重新输入您的 Chipotle 订单
  • /独立网站上的 chipotle
  • 创作者：贝壳鲨

# /版权页

描述网站如何制作、使用哪些工具、支持哪些技术的页面
  • /IndieWeb 上的版权页
  • 创作者：塞缪尔·帕尔默
概述社区内容及其价值观的页面
  • 创作者：亚当·纽博尔德

# /音乐会

您去过的音乐会的列表。
  • /IndieWeb 上的音乐会
  • @lachlanjc/音乐会
  • @jaspermayone/音乐会
人们与您联系的方式
  • /IndieWeb 上的联系方式

类似于 /你好

# /日列表

过去为您创建的 Spotify 日列表列表
  • /IndieWeb 上的日列表
  • 创作者：詹姆斯

# /death

一个页面，其中包含您去世时为家人和朋友提供的说明
  • 创建者： toby3d

# /默认值

用于执行各种任务的应用程序列表，通常基于半球视图 097 中的列表
  • 创建者：半球视图
  • 目录：defaults.rknight.me

也可能被 /uses 覆盖

#/残疾

描述您的残疾及其对您的影响的页面
  • 创作者：文森特·拉门斯

# /其他地方或/外观

非自己网站上的输出或著作列表
  • /IndieWeb 上的其他地方
  • 创作者：德克兰·奇德洛

# /follow 或 /subscribe 或 /feeds

列出您的 RSS/Atom/JSON 提要以及订阅帖子的其他方式的页面
  • /在 IndieWeb 上关注

# /for 或 /dedicated

作者向其亲人致敬或感谢影响或支持该作品的人的页面
  • 创建者： 比诺

# /glossary 或 /lexicon 或 /phrased
